INTRODUCTION
This file is the canonical, machine-optimized knowledgebase for the InfraWeaver-platform repository. It summarizes architecture, code responsibilities, operation runbooks, places to change or extend functionality, and verification commands. It is structured for programmatic parsing and human operators.

SECTION A — ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW
- Goal: Provision a Talos-based Kubernetes cluster on Proxmox, then bootstrap platform components (ArgoCD) so applications are delivered via GitOps.
- Layers:
  1. Infrastructure (Proxmox VMs) — handled by Terraform + proxmox provider.
  2. OS & bootstrap (Talos) — Talos images/config applied per-node.
  3. Cluster control (kubeconfig) — produced by talos-cluster module.
  4. Platform (ArgoCD + apps) — installed by platform-bootstrap module and reconciled from kubernetes/ manifests.
  5. CI (GitHub Actions) — runs plans and applies via OpenTofu and manages SOPS decryption.

SECTION B — DETAILED FILE MAP & RESPONSIBILITIES
- terraform/providers.tf
  - Purpose: provider pinning and auth configuration. Key providers: bpg/proxmox, siderolabs/talos, hashicorp/helm, hashicorp/kubernetes. Tokens and endpoints are injected via variables.
  - Where to edit: change versions or provider configuration here; propagate secrets via envs/<env>/terraform.tfvars or CI secrets.

- terraform/main.tf
  - Purpose: root composition. Reads envs/<env>/cluster.yaml into locals, wires modules.talos_cluster and modules.platform_bootstrap, and manages write-out of kubeconfig using local_sensitive_file.
  - Important: ordering ensures kubeconfig is generated before platform-bootstrap uses helm/kubernetes providers.

- terraform/modules/talos-cluster/
  - Responsibilities: create proxmox VMs per node record, prepare Talos machine config (cloud-init style or talosctl), wait for node readiness, collect kubeconfig and expose outputs.
  - Key outputs: kubeconfig (string), talosconfig, node_ip_map, machine_secrets.
  - Extensibility point: add node-specific cloud-init templates, or add additional provisioner null_resources to integrate with external systems.

- terraform/modules/platform-bootstrap/
  - Responsibilities: install ArgoCD via helm_release and create ApplicationSet/Application definitions that target ./kubernetes/ path.
  - Important variables: git_repo_url, git_ref, argocd_chart_version. Adjust these to point ArgoCD to different Git roots.

- envs/<env>/cluster.yaml
  - Purpose: source-of-truth for cluster topology. Each node: name, role {controlplane,worker}, cpu, memory_mb, disk_gb, storage_pool, proxmox_node_target.
  - To add nodes: edit here and re-run terraform apply. If custom device passthrough or GPU needed, leverage module inputs or extend proxmox module.

- .github/workflows/platform.yml
  - Purpose: CI flow for PR plan and main deploy. Steps include: setup (install tofu/sops/talosctl/helm), decrypt secrets, terraform init/plan/apply, and post-actions.
  - Secrets used: PROXMOX_API_TOKEN, SOPS_AGE_KEYS, TF_BACKEND credentials (if remote state used).

- scripts/get-kubeconfig.sh
  - Quick helper to cat the generated kubeconfig or port-forward argocd for operator tasks.

SECTION C — OPERATIONAL RUNBOOKS
- Full bootstrap (operator):
  1. Ensure SOPS keys available: export SOPS_AGE_KEY or configure GitHub secret.
  2. Ensure envs/<env>/cluster.yaml populated with nodes.
  3. cd /home/runner/platform/terraform
  4. tofu init && tofu plan -var-file=../envs/<env>/terraform.tfvars
  5. tofu apply -var-file=../envs/<env>/terraform.tfvars
  6. After apply, verify envs/<env>/generated/kubeconfig exists and kubectl --kubeconfig=... get nodes shows expected nodes.
- ArgoCD access:
  - Use terraform output from platform-bootstrap for argocd server address. Example: terraform output argocd_server_url
  - Port-forward: kubectl --kubeconfig=... -n argocd port-forward svc/argocd-server 8080:443
  - Login: admin password is in argocd initial admin secret (if generated) or set via helm values.

SECTION D — HOW CI AND GITOPS INTERACT
- CI writes infrastructure changes: PR triggers plan job that posts diffs. Merge to main triggers apply job.
- ArgoCD watches kubernetes/ in this repo. To deploy an app, add its manifests to kubernetes/<app>/ or add a new Application resource via platform-bootstrap module.
- SOPS decryption: secrets.sops.yaml is decrypted in CI using SOPS_AGE_KEYS available in GitHub secrets or via a self-hosted runner with key material.

SECTION E — ADDING FEATURES & MAINTENANCE GUIDELINES
- Adding nodes: edit envs/<env>/cluster.yaml and run terraform apply.
- Replacing image versions: update talos image variable in modules/talos-cluster or in envs override; ensure datastore has updated image if proxmox uses a cached image.
- Upgrading ArgoCD/Helm charts: bump chart version in platform-bootstrap variables and run terraform apply; verify ArgoCD reconcile.
- Adding pre-provision hooks: add a new null_resource in talos-cluster that runs local-exec or remote-exec to run scripts before Talos apply.

SECTION F — IMPORTANT CODE LOCATIONS (for automated parsers)
- Provider pins: terraform/providers.tf
- Root orchestration: terraform/main.tf
- Talos VM templates and provisioning: terraform/modules/talos-cluster/
- Platform bootstrap / ArgoCD: terraform/modules/platform-bootstrap/
- GitOps manifests: kubernetes/
- CI workflows: .github/workflows/platform.yml

SECTION G — VERIFICATION & MAINTENANCE COMMANDS (scriptable)
- Verify providers: sed -n '1,120p' terraform/providers.tf
- List modules: grep -R "module \"" terraform | sed -n '1,200p'
- Ensure kubeconfig exists: test -f envs/<env>/generated/kubeconfig && echo OK || echo MISSING
- Compare local to remote key files: diff -u terraform/providers.tf <(curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Werewolf-p/InfraWeaver-platform/HEAD/terraform/providers.tf)

SECTION H — SECURITY & SOPS
- Secrets live in envs/<env>/secrets.sops.yaml. Use sops with age keys for encryption. CI must provide decryption keys via secrets or mounted keyring on self-hosted runner.
- Proxmox API tokens: prefer Automation@pam tokens and scoped tokens with minimal privileges.

SECTION I — FUTURE AUTOMATION (recommendations)
- Add a regenerate_catalogs.sh script in .github/ that runs the scanning agent, diffs, and updates this index automatically when files change. Hook it into CI as a scheduled job.
- Configure homelab-iac-agent to read this file first: see next steps offered below for agent update.

APPEND: next actions available (select one):
- "commit_indexes" : commit these files into repos with proper git metadata and Co-authored-by trailer.
- "expand_with_excerpts" : append line-level excerpts and brief summaries for every file (large output).
- "configure_agent" : update homelab-iac-agent configuration to consult these files when answering or scanning.

END OF PLATFORM COMPLETE CATALOG
